title: Freeing Memory
breadcrumbs: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Freeing Memory You can use memory efficiently by using appropriate component implementation techniques and configuring the application and servers appropriately. Using handles allows Uniface to help with automatic garbage collection. Component Implementation When constructing components, consider the following: Drop forms from memory as soon as they are no longer required Delete instances of services that are no longer needed Use limited forms with their reduced field lists Clear entities as soon as the hitlist is no longer needed Free cursors and locks as soon as they are no longer required For more information, see Performance Guidelines for Component Logic and Implementation . Server Configuration Memory problems can occur in situations where a separate server process is started for each client process, and the Uniface Router is running in shared mode. You can make server configuration more flexible by using separate assignment files, for example, database.asn for the DBMS server, sasv.asn for the application server, and env.asn for the overall environment.
